sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! When I extended the path from the player's current position (rows 37-40), the green target reappeared at the end of the path! The green block is now at the end of the path (rows 37-40, cols 42-45).
So the mechanic is:
1. When the player is at the same position as a collected block, extending the path causes that target to reappear at the end of the path.
2. This allows me to "re-collect" the target in a different position.
So to fix the order, I need to:
1. Move the player to the green position (rows 37-40) - already done
2. Extend the path to reappear the green target - already done
3. Retract the path to "uncollect" the green target
4. Move the player to the blue position (rows 31-34)
5. Extend the path to collect the blue target (which will now be at rows 31-34)
6. Retract the path
7. Move the player to the green position (rows 37-40)
8. Extend the path to collect the green target (which will now be at rows 37-40)
9. Retract the path
Wait, but the green target is now at the end of the path. If I retract the path, the green target will be "uncollected" and will reappear on the right side. Then I can collect it again when the player is at the correct position.
Let me try retracting the path to see if the green target is "uncollected".

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Retract path to uncollect green target
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
